sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Avalon Globocare (NASDAQ: ALBT) wins approval for reverse split and share issuances
feed.summary: Avalon Globocare stockholders have approved several proposals, including the issuance of shares related to convertible notes and a bridge note. They also approved the exchange of Series D Preferred Stock for common shares and the issuance of restricted shares under a consulting agreement. Crucially, the board gained authority to implement a reverse stock split at a ratio between 1-for-2 and 1-for-25 without reducing authorized shares.

feed.title: Aktis Oncology Announces FDA Clearance of Investigational New Drug Applications for AKY-2519 and Provides Business Updates and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Aktis Oncology announced that the FDA cleared Investigational New Drug (IND) applications for AKY-2519, a miniprotein radioconjugate targeting B7-H3, enabling a Phase 1b clinical trial to begin in mid-2026. The company also provided business updates, including the Fast Track designation for its lead candidate AKY-1189, and reported financial results for 2025, showing increased R&D expenses and a net loss of $63.7 million. With a pro forma cash position of $562.1 million after its January 2026 IPO, Aktis anticipates funding operations into 2029.

feed.title: Allient (ALNT) VP trims stake with 6,000-share, $63 stock sale
feed.summary: Allient Inc.'s VP and Group President, Ashish Bendre, sold 6,000 shares of the company's common stock in an open-market transaction at $63.00 per share, totaling $378,000. Following this sale, Bendre directly holds 20,197.975 shares and indirectly holds an additional 1,155 shares through an ESOP Trust. The transaction is categorized as a high-impact filing with a negative sentiment, signaling a reduction in insider ownership.

feed.title: YPF Buys Back US$8 Million of Class XXX Notes Ahead of July 2026 Maturity
feed.summary: YPF Sociedad Anonima repurchased Class XXX notes totaling US$7.99 million between March 26 and March 30, 2026. This buyback, at 99.17% of par, is part of its active liability management strategy ahead of the notes' July 2026 maturity. The move aims to strengthen the company's balance sheet for bondholders and other stakeholders.

feed.title: ZenaTech Developing Proprietary Quantum Computing Hardware Platform for Defense, Infrastructure and Drone Fleet Projects
feed.summary: This article announces that ZenaTech is developing a proprietary quantum computing hardware platform. This platform is specifically designed for applications in defense, critical infrastructure, and drone fleet management projects. The development aims to leverage quantum computing's capabilities for these sensitive and high-demand sectors.

feed.title: 3 Reasons to Avoid XRAY and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
feed.summary: This article analyzes Dentsply Sirona (XRAY) and advises investors to avoid it due to declining constant currency revenue, previous growth initiatives losing money, and new investments failing to improve its Return on Invested Capital (ROIC). The company's stock is down 9.6% over the last six months, and its valuation, while seemingly cheap, is considered risky given its shaky fundamentals. Instead, the author suggests considering "one of Charlie Munger's all-time favorite businesses" and other market-beating stocks identified by StockStory's AI platform.

feed.title: YD Bio (YDES) director files Form 3 with no share activity
feed.summary: YD Bio Ltd director Lee Jerry Yin-Chia has filed a Form 3 insider report for YDES, indicating no reported purchases, sales, or other insider transactions. The filing lists him as a director, with all transaction counts and share totals recorded as zero. This Form 3 filing confirms no insider trading activity from the specified director.

feed.title: [144] Xeris Biopharma Holdings, Inc. SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article details a Form 144 SEC filing by Xeris Biopharma Holdings, Inc. (XERS) dated March 30, 2026. The filing indicates a proposed sale of 10,834 common shares with an aggregate market value of $58,503.60, acquired as Restricted Stock Units on March 28, 2026. The sale is planned through Morgan Stanley Smith Barney LLC on NASDAQ.

feed.title: EV charging in days, no trenching: Xos targets facilities teams
feed.summary: Xos Inc. (NASDAQ: XOS) will showcase its mobile energy solutions at the Southern California Facilities Expo, April 8–9, 2026, targeting facilities managers with grid-independent EV charging that installs quickly without utility upgrades. The platform aims to protect energy budgets through dynamic load management and assist with WAIRE compliance. Xos emphasizes its solutions avoid common delays and costs associated with traditional EV infrastructure, having been deployed across over 100 global sites.

feed.title: WPP Establishes New U.S. Debt Indenture Framework for Future Note Issuances
feed.summary: WPP has established a new base indenture for U.S. dollar debt securities through a Form 6-K filing on March 30, 2026. This framework enhances the company's flexibility for future note issuances and strengthens protections for U.S. bond investors, leveraging legal opinions from New York, English, and Jersey law. The move streamlines WPP's capital markets infrastructure, allowing efficient access to U.S. investors and standardizing terms for future guaranteed notes, with Wilmington Trust and Citibank involved as trustee and registrar/paying agent, respectively.

feed.title: WisdomTree (NYSE: WT) raises $603.75M via 4.50% convertible notes due 2031
feed.summary: WisdomTree, Inc. has successfully raised $603.75 million through a private offering of 4.50% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031, yielding net proceeds of approximately $591.2 million. These notes are senior unsecured obligations maturing in October 2031 and are initially convertible at about $21.58 per share, with provisions for a higher conversion rate under specific corporate events. The issuance introduces new debt alongside existing convertible notes, providing financial flexibility but also potential future equity dilution.

feed.title: ITW Remains Top Trading Value Despite 1.1% Drop and Insider Selling Spree
feed.summary: Shares of Illinois Tool Works (ITW) fell 1.10% on March 30, 2026, despite strong Q4 2025 earnings and optimistic 2026 guidance, while remaining a top trading value. The decline was accompanied by significant insider selling and mixed analyst sentiment, although institutional investors increased their holdings. The company demonstrates strong profitability and cash flow but faces concerns regarding its debt-to-equity ratio and market reassessment in the industrial sector.

feed.title: Assessing Ziff Davis (ZD) Valuation After Removal From The NASDAQ Internet Index
feed.summary: Ziff Davis (ZD) has been removed from the NASDAQ Internet Index following a significant short-term share price increase. While one valuation model suggests ZD is 4% undervalued due to digital content and SaaS growth, its current P/E ratio of 33.5x is considerably higher than industry averages, indicating potential overvaluation and less room for error. Investors are encouraged to consider both the growth narrative and valuation risks.

feed.title: Fire protection firm buys 20,000 sq. ft. Somerset facility from PepsiCo
feed.summary: Approved Fire Protection has acquired a 20,000 sq. ft. industrial facility in Somerset, New Jersey, from PepsiCo. This former PepsiCo and Frito Lay site will become a regional distribution and service hub, strategically located to support the company's growth across the Northeast. The acquisition leverages the property's infrastructure and connectivity to major transportation routes, enabling Approved Fire Protection to expand its 90-year legacy of service to a growing customer base.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Co. stock faces analyst downgrades and margin pressures amid shifting consumer trends
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. (CPB) is experiencing analyst downgrades from Deutsche Bank and UBS due to margin pressures in its snacks segment and competitive headwinds despite reaffirming its Q2 2026 guidance. The company, a North American packaged food leader, is navigating shifting consumer preferences towards healthier options while emphasizing snacks growth and cost-saving initiatives. Investors are watching for margin recovery in Q3/Q4 and the stability of its dividend amidst these challenges.

feed.title: Wall Street Veteran Behind America’s First XRP ETF Says ‘XRP Community Is an Army,’ Says He’s Still Bullish
feed.summary: Sal Gilbertie, CEO of Teucrium, which launched America's first XRP ETF, describes the XRP community as an "army" following the fund's $500 million intake in 12 weeks. He remains bullish on Ripple due to its focus on fast global money movement and aggressive expansion. Gilbertie also emphasizes the importance of regulatory clarity for the crypto sector.

feed.title: FPL Awards Grants To Six Nonprofits In Escambia County
feed.summary: Florida Power & Light Company (FPL) has awarded grants through its NextEra Energy Foundation to six nonprofits in Escambia County. These grants aim to support various community initiatives including early childhood readiness, literacy, youth leadership, workforce development, grief counseling, and homelessness services. The recipient organizations are ReadyKids!, Central Gulf Coast Freedom Schools, Str8 Up Leadership Academy, Waterfront Rescue Mission, Ronald McDonald House Charities, and Valerie’s House.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reconsider Pfizer (PFE) After Diverging Valuation Narratives?
feed.summary: This article examines Pfizer's (PFE) valuation through two approaches: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio, both indicating Pfizer is currently undervalued. It then introduces "Narratives"—bullish and bearish case scenarios with differing fair values and assumptions for revenue, margins, and pipeline success—to help investors frame their own valuation perspective.

feed.title: Astera Labs Stock Plummets 19% With 5-Day Losing Streak
feed.summary: Astera Labs stock has experienced a significant downturn, plummeting 19% and extending a five-day losing streak. The article provides the headline and indicates a substantial drop in the company's stock performance. No further details about the reasons for the drop are available in this content.

feed.title: EOG Resources Stock 6-Day Winning Spree: Stock Climbs 8.0%
feed.summary: EOG Resources stock has experienced a six-day winning streak, resulting in an 8.0% climb. The article seems to be an excerpt from a financial analysis platform, Trefis, and mentions data sources and terms of use. However, most of the content consists of navigation and site-related information rather than detailed stock analysis.

feed.title: Rosen Law Firm Files Securities Class Action Against Aldeyra Therapeutics
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm has filed a securities class action lawsuit against Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. on behalf of investors who purchased the company's stock between January 1, 2025, and March 15, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Aldeyra Therapeutics made false and misleading statements about the results of clinical trials for one of its drug candidates, leading to artificial inflation of its stock price. This case highlights the importance of transparency and accurate disclosures for publicly traded pharmaceutical firms.

feed.title: Lindsey Oil Refinery worker's sadness ahead of final shift
feed.summary: A pipe fitter, David Cook, is expressing sadness as he prepares for his final shift at Lindsey Oil Refinery in North Killingholme. The refinery was taken over by an official receiver in June 2025 following its owner Prax's collapse and will be bought by Phillips 66. Cook, 63, worked for contractors at the site for 14 years and is among many facing job loss, though some employees have been offered roles with Phillips 66 or retained temporarily.

feed.title: GeneDx (WGS) CEO exercises 80K RSUs, sells 47K shares for taxes
feed.summary: GeneDx Holdings Corp. CEO Katherine Stueland exercised 80,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) and subsequently sold 46,933 shares to cover tax withholding obligations. These "sell to cover" transactions were non-discretionary and executed at weighted average prices between $60.93 and $65.61 per share. After these transactions, the CEO beneficially owns 91,514 shares of Class A common stock, along with additional RSUs and stock options.

feed.title: GeneDx (WGS) CFO exercises 16,250 RSUs, sells shares for taxes
feed.summary: GeneDx Holdings Corp.'s CFO, Kevin Feeley, exercised 16,250 restricted stock units (RSUs) and subsequently sold 8,818 Class A Common Stock shares. These sales, conducted at weighted average prices between $60s and $65s per share, were explicitly for covering tax withholding obligations associated with the RSU vesting, categorized as a "sell to cover" transaction rather than a discretionary trade. Following these transactions, Feeley beneficially owns 34,580 Class A Common Stock shares, with additional contingent rights to 93,173 RSU shares and options for 25,906 shares.

feed.title: Raymond James Maintains Weave Communications(WEAV.US) With Buy Rating
feed.summary: This article provides details on Raymond James maintaining its Buy rating for Weave Communications (WEAV.US). The report also includes the price target set by the analyst firm.

feed.title: ZenaTech: A big bet on the rise of AI drones and drones-as-a-service
feed.summary: ZenaTech is undergoing a significant transformation, shifting from agricultural software to AI drones and Drone as a Service (DaaS), which now accounts for approximately 70% of its revenue after recent acquisitions. While the company is growing rapidly through acquiring land surveying and engineering firms, it remains early-stage, loss-making, and has a complex capital structure with high dilution risk. The investment case hinges on its ability to evolve from a services roll-up into a scalable tech platform, capitalizing on a supportive market for commercial drones.

feed.title: WEBTOON (NASDAQ: WBTN) sets salary and equity for its president
feed.summary: WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. disclosed the compensation package for its President, Yongsoo Kim, through an amended 8-K filing. Mr. Kim's compensation includes an annual base salary of approximately $500,000, a $500,000 short-term incentive opportunity, and long-term equity incentives comprising $750,000 each in restricted stock units and performance stock units. These terms were formalized in an amended long-term assignment letter effective March 2, 2026, and approved by the Board on March 26, 2026.

feed.title: Washington Trust Bancorp (WASH) Initiated at Neutral by Piper Sandler amid Capital Constraints
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has initiated coverage of Washington Trust Bancorp (NASDAQ: WASH) with a Neutral rating and a $32 price target, citing capital constraints that limit growth and push share buybacks lower on the priority list. Despite this, the company is making strategic investments in Wealth Management and Commercial Banking, including acquisitions and new hires, and plans to open a new branch in Pawtucket, Rhode Island. The bank reported strong Q4 2025 results with increased net income and net interest income, and an improved net interest margin due to a better funding mix.

feed.title: Insider sells multiple VTS shares reported in Form 144 (NYSE: VTS)
feed.summary: Vitesse Energy (NYSE: VTS) recently reported insider sales via Form 144, detailing multiple open-market dispositions by Brian Cree, including sales of 43,216 shares on January 14, 2026, and 19,824 shares on January 16, 2026. The filing indicates these are routine dispositions with no clear material signal or offering, and the proceeds go to the seller rather than the issuer. As of March 30, 2026, VTS had 39,776,727 shares outstanding.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Initiates Coverage on WAY: Equal-Weight, $28 PT
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has initiated coverage on Waystar Holding (WAY) with an Equal-Weight rating and a price target of $28. This new analyst coverage adds to existing ratings, with other firms like Deutsche Bank and Wells Fargo having previously issued Buy or Overweight ratings with varying price targets. Waystar Holding is a cloud technology provider for healthcare organizations, and the average target price from 21 analysts is $36.52, implying a significant upside from its current price.

feed.title: VUG ETF Declines 0.6%
feed.summary: This article reports that the VUG ETF declined by 0.6%. This brief update highlights the fund's performance.

feed.title: VERI: HC Wainwright Reiterates Buy Rating and $9 Price Target
feed.summary: HC Wainwright & Co. has reiterated its Buy rating for Veritone, Inc. (VERI), maintaining a price target of $9.00 USD. This aligns with a history of consistent Buy ratings from various analysts for the AI computing solutions provider. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $9.63 for VERI, suggesting a significant upside from its current share price.

feed.title: Walker & Dunlop Arranges Joint Venture Equity Partnership and Construction Financing for $132 Million Multifamily Development in Richmond's Scott's Addition District
feed.summary: Walker & Dunlop has facilitated a joint venture and secured construction financing for a $132 million multifamily development in Richmond, Virginia's Scott's Addition District. The project, a redevelopment of the former Greyhound Bus Station, will deliver 386 Class A residences and over 14,000 square feet of retail. This initiative aims to enhance the rapidly growing neighborhood with a world-class residential experience, urban retail, and extensive amenities.

feed.title: WD Investors Have Opportunity to Join Walker & Dunlop, Inc. Fraud Investigation with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is investigating potential securities law violations by Walker & Dunlop, Inc. following a significant drop in its adjusted earnings per share for Q4 2025, which were nearly 81% below consensus estimates. This news led to a more than 19.4% decline in the company's stock on the day of the announcement. Shareholders who incurred losses are encouraged to contact the firm for a fraud investigation.

feed.title: Is WD-40’s (WDFC) Bigger Dividend and Specialist Push Redefining Its Capital Allocation Playbook?
feed.summary: WD-40 Company recently declared a higher quarterly dividend of US$1.02 per share and highlighted the growth of its higher-margin Specialist offerings and e-commerce channels. This move suggests a continued focus on shareholder returns while the company invests in premium products and digital platforms. The article examines whether this increased dividend and emphasis on specialist products redefine WD-40’s investment strategy, considering potential risks like cost inflation and slower traction in new initiatives.

feed.title: VRME: Revenue fell sharply in 2025 amid a shipping partner transition, but gross margin improved
feed.summary: VerifyMe, Inc. (VRME) experienced a sharp decline in revenue and customer base in 2025 due to a significant shipping partner transition. Despite this, the company saw an improvement in gross margin and an increase in Adjusted EBITDA year-over-year. VRME is currently working on a merger and is focused on enhancing its operational efficiency.

feed.title: Director James Caruso receives 40,000 VTAK options at $1.15 exercise price
feed.summary: Catheter Precision, Inc. director James Joseph Caruso received a grant of 40,000 stock options (VTAK) with an exercise price of $1.15 per share. These options are set to expire on March 26, 2036, and will vest entirely on the 180-day anniversary of the grant date. This transaction was reported via an SEC Form 4 filing and represents a compensation-related award rather than an open-market purchase.

feed.title: Catheter Precision (VTAK) director receives 40,000 stock options at $1.15 strike
feed.summary: Catheter Precision director Arno Andrew was granted 40,000 stock options with an exercise price of $1.15 per share, expiring on March 26, 2036. The options vest 100% after 180 days from the grant date, representing a standard equity incentive for director compensation. This transaction is categorized as a grant or award rather than an open-market purchase or sale.

feed.title: [6-K] VOX ROYALTY CORP. Current Report (Foreign Issuer)
feed.summary: Vox Royalty Corp. (VOXR) filed a Form 6-K, a current report for foreign private issuers, with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on March 30, 2026. The report indicates that Vox Royalty Corp. is a foreign private issuer submitting annual reports under cover of Form 40-F. The filing includes a press release as Exhibit 99.1.

feed.title: VWO ETF Declines 0.1%
feed.summary: The Vanguard FTSE Emerging Markets ETF (VWO) experienced a slight decline of 0.1% in trading on the New York Stock Exchange. The ETF had an average daily volume of 18,367,314 shares and is currently trading near its 52-week low.

feed.title: Short Interest in Valhi, Inc. (NYSE:VHI) Grows By 24.3%
feed.summary: Short interest in Valhi, Inc. (NYSE:VHI) significantly increased by 24.3% in March to 67,454 shares, representing a 3.1-day ratio of short interest and about 0.2% of the company's shares sold short. This rise comes after Valhi reported a substantial quarterly earnings miss, posting an EPS of ($1.86) against a $0.86 consensus, alongside negative net margin and return on equity. The company declared a $0.08 quarterly dividend, while analysts maintain a consensus "Sell" rating with a $12 price target, contrasting with the current trading price around $14.22.

feed.title: Truist cuts Vornado Realty stock price target on lower FFO estimate
feed.summary: Truist Securities has lowered its price target for Vornado Realty Trust (NYSE:VNO) to $28 from $29, while maintaining a Hold rating. This adjustment follows a reduction in Truist's 2026 FFO estimate for Vornado to $2.25 per share, below the consensus, due to seasonality in signage revenue and other factors. Despite the stock trading near its 52-week low and a 38% decline over six months, InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be undervalued, and the company offers a 2.99% dividend yield with a 36-year history of payments.

feed.title: Village Farms Extends FCC Credit Facility to 2031, Cuts Interest Margin by 50 Bps
feed.summary: Village Farms International has amended its credit agreement with Farm Credit Canada, extending the maturity to February 3, 2031, and reducing the interest margin by 50 basis points. This move is expected to decrease borrowing costs and improve the company's financial flexibility. The updated terms support Village Farms' long-term liquidity needs.

feed.title: Village Farms cuts loan rate and gains four extra years on $15.4M debt
feed.summary: Village Farms International (NASDAQ: VFF) has amended and extended its Farm Credit Canada loan, reducing the interest rate by 50 basis points and extending the maturity date by four years to February 3, 2031. The variable-rate loan has a current balance of US $15.4 million, with an interest rate currently below 7.0%. Management views this amendment as a reflection of a strong lender relationship and a supportive step for the company's growth strategy in 2026.
